html:
𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄

THE ANCIENT COVENANT RECORD

Volume Thirty-One — Complete

The Community Rule

SEREKH HAYACHAD — 1QS

The Foundational Governing Document of the Qumran Community

Columns 1 through 11

The Men of the Community Who Freely Volunteer to Turn From All Evil — The Two Spirits in the Heart of Man — All the Glory of Adam Shall Be Theirs — In the Wilderness Prepare the Way of YHWH — My Justification Is With Creator

PRIMARY MANUSCRIPT AUTHORITIES

Dead Sea Scrolls — 1QS Cave 1 Primary — 4QS — 5QS

DSS Primary Authority — No Christian or Rabbinic Framework

YHWH and The Creator only

COMPARATIVE NOTES AT END OF EVERY CHAPTER

DSS · ORIT GE'EZ · MASORETIC VARIANT · CRITICAL NOTE

YHWH / 𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄 and The Creator only — no substitute titles

All personal names in original Hebrew form throughout

SEREKH HAYACHAD — THE COMMUNITY RULE — THE THEOLOGICAL FOUNDATION — COLUMNS 1 THROUGH 4

COMMUNITY RULE — COLUMN 1 The Rule for the Community — To Seek Creator With the Whole Heart — To Separate From All the Men of Falsehood — To Enter the Covenant Before Creator — To Walk Blamelessly in All His Ways — The Maskil Shall Instruct All the Sons of Light

1 The Rule for the Community. This is the order for the men of his community who freely volunteer to turn from all evil and to hold fast to all that he has commanded according to his will.

2 To separate themselves from the congregation of the men of falsehood and to become a community in Torah and in property. And they shall answer according to the sons of Tzadok, the priests who keep the covenant.

3 And according to the decision of the majority of the men of their community who hold fast to the covenant: on their authority the decision shall be taken in every matter of Torah and property and judgment.

4 To practice truth together and humility and righteousness and justice and loving-kindness and walking humbly in all their ways.

5 No man shall walk in the stubbornness of his heart to go astray after his heart and his eyes and the inclination of his evil impulse.

6 Rather he shall circumcise in the community the foreskin of his impulse and his stiff neck, to lay a foundation of truth for Yisra'EL, for the community of the everlasting covenant.

7 To atone for all those who freely volunteer for holiness in Aharon and for the house of truth in Yisra'EL and those who join them for the community and for controversy and judgment to condemn all those who transgress the statute.

8 These are the foundations of the spirit for the sons of truth in the world. The visitation of all those who walk in this spirit will be healing, great peace in a long life, bearing fruit with all everlasting blessings and everlasting joy in life without end.

9 And a crown of glory with a robe of majesty in everlasting light. But the visitation of all those who walk in the spirit of deceit will be an abundance of afflictions at the hands of all the angels of destruction.

10 Everlasting damnation in the wrath of Creator's fury, everlasting terror and perpetual shame with the disgrace of destruction by the fire of the dark regions.

11 And all their times shall be for generation upon generation of grief and bitter misery with dark disasters until they are destroyed without remnant or survivor for them.

12 These are the foundations of these two spirits. In them is the origin of all the sons of truth and all the sons of deceit.

13 The nature of all the children of men is ruled by these two spirits, and during their life all the hosts of men have a portion in their divisions and walk in both their ways.

14 The spirit of truth and the spirit of deceit. Truth born in a spring of light and deceit from a well of darkness.

15 In the hand of the Prince of Light is the dominion over all the sons of righteousness. They walk in the ways of light. And in the hand of the Angel of Darkness is all dominion over the sons of deceit.

16 They walk in the ways of darkness. And through the Angel of Darkness all the sons of righteousness go astray. And all their sins and their iniquities and their guilt and their deeds of transgression are under his dominion according to the mysteries of Creator.

17 Until his appointed time. And all their afflictions and the appointed times of their distress are in the dominion of his hostility. And all the spirits of his lot cause the sons of light to stumble.

18 But the Creator of Yisra'EL and the Angel of his truth have helped all the sons of light. For he created the spirits of light and of darkness and on them he founded all deeds.

19 One spirit Creator loves forever and delights in all its deeds forever. The other he detests its counsel and all its ways he hates forever.

20 These are their ways in the world. To illuminate the heart of man and to make straight before him all the ways of true righteousness, and to instill in his heart the fear of the judgments of Creator.

21 A spirit of humility and slowness to anger and abundant compassion and eternal goodness and understanding and insight and mighty wisdom which believes in all the deeds of Creator and leans upon his abundant compassion.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The Community Rule — Serekh HaYachad — was found in Cave 1 at Qumran in 1947 as one of the seven original scrolls, designated 1QS. It is the most complete and best-preserved of all the Qumran sectarian documents. Additional fragments were found in Cave 4 — 4QS — and Cave 5 — 5QS. The 1QS scroll contains eleven columns of Hebrew text and is the foundational governing document of the Qumran yahad — community. Column 1 establishes the covenant theological foundation of the community's existence: the Two Spirits doctrine that frames all human experience within the conflict between the spirit of truth and the spirit of deceit.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez tradition does not preserve the Community Rule. The DSS manuscripts — 1QS primary — are the exclusive authority for this text. Column 1's Two Spirits theology is the most developed dualistic anthropology in the entire Qumran corpus: every human being carries a portion of both the spirit of light and the spirit of darkness in the covenant present age, and the ultimate resolution of this inner conflict awaits the covenant appointed time of Creator's visitation. The covenant community understands itself as walking in the way of light under the dominion of the Prince of Light.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic tradition does not contain the Community Rule. The covenant Two Spirits doctrine of Column 1 has no precise parallel in the Masoretic covenant canon, though its covenant theological foundations are rooted in the covenant created distinction between light and darkness in Bereshit 1 and the covenant prophetic covenant imagery of Yesha'yahu 45:7. The covenant community's covenant systematic covenant dualism is the most elaborate covenant theological covenant development of these covenant foundational covenant biblical covenant themes.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 15 — in the hand of the Prince of Light is the dominion over all the sons of righteousness. They walk in the ways of light. And in the hand of the Angel of Darkness is all dominion over the sons of deceit. The covenant cosmic conflict between the Prince of Light and the Angel of Darkness is the Community Rule's covenant framework for understanding the covenant community's covenant experience of both covenant internal covenant struggle and covenant external covenant persecution. The covenant sons of light are not immune to the Angel of Darkness — verse 16 — but are ultimately under the dominion of Creator's covenant protective covenant angel.

COMMUNITY RULE — COLUMN 2 The Maskil Shall Bless All Those Who Fear Creator — The Levites Shall Curse All the Men of the Lot of Belial — The Men of the Covenant Shall Answer Amen — No Man Shall Go Into the Water to Share in the Purity — They Shall Not Depart From Any Command of Creator

1 The Maskil shall bless all the men of the lot of Creator who walk blamelessly in all his ways. And he shall say: May he bless you with every good thing and keep you from every evil.

2 May he enlighten your heart with the understanding of life and grace you with everlasting knowledge. And may he lift up his gracious face toward you for everlasting peace.

3 And the Levites shall curse all the men of the lot of Belial. They shall begin and say: Be cursed for all your wicked guilty deeds.

4 May Creator give you up to terror at the hand of all the avengers of vengeance. May he visit you with destruction at the hand of all those who repay wickedness.

5 Be accursed without mercy for the darkness of your deeds and be sentenced to the gloom of everlasting fire. May Creator have no mercy on you when you call and may he not forgive to wipe away your sins.

6 May he raise the face of his anger to avenge himself upon you. And may there be no peace for you at the mouth of all who hold fast to the fathers.

7 And all those entering the covenant shall say after them: Amen, Amen.

8 And the priests and the Levites shall continue and say: Cursed be the man who enters this covenant with idols upon his heart and sets the stumbling block of his iniquity before him to fall upon it.

9 When he hears the words of this covenant he blesses himself in his heart saying: I shall have peace even though I walk in the stubbornness of my heart. But his spirit shall be swept away with the thirsty and the satiated.

10 Creator will not be willing to forgive him. Rather the anger of Creator and the zeal of his judgments shall consume him for everlasting destruction. And all the curses of this covenant shall cling to him.

11 And Creator will set him apart for evil and he will be cut off from the midst of all the sons of light because of his apostasy from Creator through his idols and the stumbling block of his iniquity.

12 He will place his lot in the midst of those accursed forever. And all those entering the covenant shall answer after them: Amen, Amen.

13 Thus shall they do year by year all the days of the dominion of Belial. The priests shall pass in review first according to their spirits, one after another. And the Levites shall pass in review after them.

14 And all the people shall pass in review in the third place, one after another, by thousands, hundreds, fifties, and tens, so that each man of Yisra'EL may know his appointed standing in the community of Creator.

15 According to the everlasting design, no man shall be moved up from his appointed standing or moved down from the place of his lot.

16 All those entering the order of the community shall pass over into the covenant before Creator to do according to all that he commanded, and shall not turn back from following him through any dread or terror or trial or fright in the dominion of Belial.

17 When they enter the covenant the priests and the Levites shall bless the Creator of salvations and all the faithful works of his truth, and all those entering the covenant shall say after them: Amen, Amen.

18 And again the priests shall recite the righteous deeds of Creator in his mighty works and proclaim all his merciful compassion toward Yisra'EL.

19 And the Levites shall recite the iniquities of the children of Yisra'EL and all their guilty transgressions and their sins during the dominion of Belial.

20 And all those entering the covenant shall confess after them and say: We have committed iniquity, we have transgressed, we have sinned, we have done wickedly, we and our fathers before us, in walking contrary to the ordinances of truth.

21 And righteous is Creator and true is his judgment against us and against our fathers. But the compassion of his steadfast love he has bestowed upon us from everlasting to everlasting.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The 1QS Cave 1 scroll confirms Column 2. The covenant entry ceremony described here — with its covenant antiphonal blessings and curses, its covenant Amen responses, and its covenant annual review — is the most elaborate covenant covenant-renewal ceremony in the Qumran corpus. The covenant ceremony parallels the covenant Deuteronomic blessing and cursing ceremony of Devarim 27-28 but is specifically adapted for the covenant community's own covenant institutional structure of priests, Levites, and people.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez covenant tradition does not preserve the Community Rule. However, the covenant confessional formula of verse 20 — we have committed iniquity, we have transgressed, we have sinned, we have done wickedly — parallels the covenant great confessional prayers of Daniyel 9, Nekhemyah 9, and Ezra 9 that the Orit Ge'ez preserves. The covenant communal covenant confession before covenant covenant renewal is the covenant established covenant pattern of covenant Second Temple covenant piety.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic Devarim 27-28 provides the covenant foundational covenant framework for the antiphonal blessing-and-curse ceremony of Column 2. The Community Rule's covenant ceremony adapts the covenant Mosaic covenant framework to the specific covenant institutional structure of the covenant Qumran community: the covenant priests and Levites perform the covenant priestly covenant functions while the covenant community as a whole responds with covenant Amen.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 9 — when he hears the words of this covenant he blesses himself in his heart saying: I shall have peace even though I walk in the stubbornness of my heart. The covenant quotation from Devarim 29:19 — the curse of the man who enters the covenant insincerely — is the Community Rule's covenant most direct covenant warning about covenant hypocritical covenant covenant membership. The covenant man who enters the covenant outwardly while maintaining covenant inner covenant rebellion receives the most severe covenant judgment: Creator will not forgive him and all the curses of the covenant shall cling to him.

COMMUNITY RULE — COLUMN 3 The Two Spirits — Creator Created the Spirits of Light and Darkness — The Prince of Light and the Angel of Darkness — All the Sons of Righteousness Walk in the Ways of Light — The Visitation of the Sons of Truth — Until the Appointed Time of His Visitation

1 For it is through the spirit of true counsel of Creator that are atoned the paths of a man, all his iniquities, so that he may look upon the light of life. And it is through the holy spirit of the community in his truth that he is cleansed from all his iniquities.

2 And through an upright and humble spirit his sin is atoned. And through the submission of his soul to all the ordinances of Creator his flesh is cleansed when he is sprinkled with water for impurity and when he sanctifies himself with water of cleanness.

3 Let him establish his steps to walk blamelessly in all the ways of Creator, as he commanded concerning the appointed times of his assemblies. He shall not turn aside to the right or to the left and shall not walk in any of these things.

4 Then he will be accepted by atonement pleasing before Creator, and it shall be for him a covenant of the everlasting community. The Maskil shall understand and teach all the sons of light the nature of all the sons of man according to all the kinds of their spirits.

5 According to their signs and according to their works in their generations and according to the visitation of their afflictions together with the times of their peace. Everything that exists and everything that happens is from the Creator of knowledge.

6 Before things came into being he established their entire design. When they come into being for their appointed testimony they fulfill their work, nothing can be changed. In his hand are the judgments of all things.

7 He it is who sustains them in all their needs. He created man to govern the world and appointed for him two spirits in which to walk until the time of his visitation: the spirits of truth and of deceit.

8 From the spring of light come the generations of truth and from the well of darkness come the generations of deceit. In the hand of the Prince of Light is dominion over all the sons of righteousness. They walk in the paths of light.

9 And in the hand of the Angel of Darkness is all dominion over the sons of deceit. They walk in the paths of darkness. Through the Angel of Darkness all the sons of righteousness go astray.

10 All their sins, their iniquities, their guilt, and their transgressions are under his dominion according to the mysteries of Creator until his appointed time and all their afflictions and the times of their distress are in the dominion of his hostility.

11 And all the spirits of his lot cause the sons of light to stumble. But the Creator of Yisra'EL and the Angel of his truth help all the sons of light. He created the spirits of light and darkness and upon them founded every work.

12 One spirit Creator loves forever and delights in all its deeds forever. The other he detests its counsel and all its ways he hates forever. These are their ways in the world.

13 To the spirit of truth belong: to enlighten the heart of man, to make level before him all the paths of true righteousness, to make his heart fear the judgments of Creator.

14 A spirit of humility and slowness to anger and great compassion and everlasting goodness and understanding and insight and mighty wisdom which believes in all the works of Creator and rests upon his great compassion.

15 A spirit of knowledge in every plan of action, of zeal for the judgments of righteousness and of holy thought with firm purpose and great charity toward all the sons of truth and glorious purity loathing all unclean idols.

16 Walking humbly with prudence in all things, concealing the truth of the mysteries of knowledge.

17 To the spirit of deceit belong: greed and slackness in righteous deeds, wickedness and falsehood, pride and haughtiness and lying, deception and cruelty and great ungodliness, hastiness and abundance of iniquity.

18 Brazenness and great lewdness and the way of abomination in the service of uncleanness and a blasphemous tongue, blindness of eye and dullness of ear and stiffness of neck and hardness of heart in walking in all the ways of darkness.

19 And the visitation of all those who walk in it will be an abundance of afflictions at the hands of all the angels of destruction in the everlasting Pit by the furious wrath of the Creator of vengeance.

20 Everlasting terror and perpetual shame with the disgrace of destruction in the fire of the regions of darkness. And all their times for generation upon generation in grief and bitter misery.

21 In dark disasters until they are destroyed having no remnant or survivor. These are the foundations of these two spirits and upon them is founded every act of man until the time of the final visitation.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The 1QS Cave 1 scroll confirms Column 3. This column contains the fullest exposition of the Two Spirits doctrine in the entire Qumran corpus — an elaborate covenant theological covenant anthropology that attributes every human characteristic and action to the dominion of either the spirit of truth or the spirit of deceit. The doctrine does not deny human freedom but frames it within the cosmic sovereignty of Creator who created both spirits and appointed the appointed time for the final resolution of their conflict.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez tradition does not preserve the Community Rule. The covenant catalogue of virtues belonging to the spirit of truth — verses 13-16: humility, slowness to anger, great compassion, understanding, mighty wisdom — parallels the covenant ethical catalogue of the Orit Ge'ez wisdom tradition, particularly as preserved in Mishlei and the wisdom passages of Chanokh. The covenant connection between the Two Spirits doctrine and the covenant wisdom tradition reflects the covenant community's roots in the covenant wisdom covenant theological framework.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic tradition provides the covenant foundational covenant texts that the Two Spirits doctrine develops. Bereshit 1's covenant light and darkness, Devarim 30:15-20's covenant two ways of life and death, and Yesha'yahu 45:7's covenant creation of both light and darkness all contribute to the Community Rule's covenant systematic covenant dualism. The covenant doctrine is the most elaborate covenant systematic covenant development of these covenant foundational covenant biblical covenant themes in the Qumran literature.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 7 — he created man to govern the world and appointed for him two spirits in which to walk until the time of his visitation: the spirits of truth and of deceit. The covenant creational grounding of the Two Spirits doctrine: the covenant two spirits are not cosmic accidents or the product of covenant rebellion but the covenant deliberate covenant creative covenant act of Creator who appointed both for the covenant governance of covenant human covenant life until the covenant final covenant visitation. The covenant doctrine is fundamentally theocentric — the covenant resolution of the covenant conflict belongs entirely to Creator's covenant appointed covenant time.

COMMUNITY RULE — COLUMN 4 The Visitation of the Sons of Truth — Healing and Great Peace in a Long Life — The Visitation of the Sons of Deceit — Everlasting Damnation — Creator Has Appointed an End for Deceit — Until Now the Spirits of Truth and Deceit Battle in the Heart of Man — The Elect of Grace

1 And the visitation of all those who walk in this spirit will be healing and great peace in a long life, and bearing fruit with all everlasting blessings and everlasting joy in perpetual life.

2 And a crown of glory with a garment of majesty in everlasting light. But the visitation of all those who walk in the spirit of deceit will be an abundance of afflictions at the hands of all the angels of destruction.

3 Everlasting damnation in the wrath of Creator's fury, terror and perpetual shame with the disgrace of destruction in the fire of the dark regions.

4 And all their times shall be for generation upon generation of grief and bitter misery in dark disasters until they are destroyed without remnant or survivor for them.

5 The nature of all mankind is in these two spirits. And according to a man's portion in them, whether great or small, so is a man favored or despised all the days of his life.

6 For Creator has set these two spirits in equal measure until the final age, and he has placed everlasting enmity between their divisions.

7 Falsehood is an abomination to truth and truth is an abomination to falsehood. Fierce strife is in all their judgments for they do not walk together.

8 And Creator in the mysteries of his understanding and in his glorious wisdom has ordained an end for the existence of deceit. And at the appointed time of the visitation he will destroy it forever.

9 Then truth shall arise in the world forever, for it has been defiled in the paths of wickedness during the dominion of deceit until the time of the appointed judgment.

10 Then Creator will refine in his truth all the deeds of man and purify for himself some of mankind, removing all spirit of deceit from within his flesh and cleansing him with a holy spirit from all wicked deeds.

11 He will sprinkle upon him a spirit of truth like purifying water to cleanse him from all the abominations of falsehood and from the defilement of the unclean spirit.

12 Thus the upright shall understand knowledge of the Most High and the wisdom of the sons of heaven shall be made perfect. For Creator has chosen them for an everlasting covenant.

13 And all the glory of Adam shall be theirs. There shall be no more deceit and all the works of falsehood shall be put to shame. Until now the spirits of truth and of deceit battle in the heart of every man.

14 They walk in wisdom and folly. And according to his portion of truth and righteousness does a man hate deceit and according to his portion in the lot of deceit does he abhor truth.

15 For Creator has set them in equal measure until the appointed end and the new creation. And he knows the result of their works for all the times of the age.

16 He has given them to the sons of man as a heritage together with the lots of the holy ones. And he has made the sons of man inheritors of the lot of light according to their knowledge.

17 And he has fixed the times of peace for the sons of truth and the times of the dominion of deceit for the sons of deceit as well as the assigned lots of both.

18 These are the counsels of the spirit for the sons of truth in the world. And the visitation of all those who walk in this spirit will be the healing of great peace in a long life.

19 And bearing much fruit, all the blessings of eternity, everlasting joy in perpetual life and a crown of glory and a robe of majesty in eternal light.

20 But the visitation of all those who walk in the spirit of deceit will be an abundance of afflictions at the hands of all the angels of destruction, eternal damnation in the fury of Creator's vengeance.

21 Perpetual terror and everlasting shame with the disgrace of destruction in the fire of the dark regions, and all times for their generations of grief and bitter misery and dark calamities until none of them remain. This is the end of the two spirits.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The 1QS Cave 1 scroll confirms Column 4. The column culminates the Two Spirits exposition begun in Columns 3-4 with the covenant eschatological covenant resolution: at the appointed time of the covenant final covenant visitation — verse 8-12 — Creator will destroy deceit forever, refine all human deeds in his truth, sprinkle a spirit of truth like purifying water upon the elect, and restore all the glory of Adam — verse 13. The covenant eschatological covenant promise of verse 13 echoes the Damascus Document's identical promise and establishes it as the covenant central covenant hope of the covenant Qumran community.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez covenant tradition preserves the covenant glory of Adam theology — including in Chanokh — which the Community Rule cites in verse 13 as the covenant ultimate covenant eschatological covenant destiny of the covenant elect. The covenant restoration of Adamic covenant glory as the covenant highest covenant eschatological covenant promise connects the Community Rule to the covenant broader covenant Qumran covenant theological tradition and to the covenant Ethiopian Orit Ge'ez covenant canonical tradition through Chanokh.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic tradition provides the covenant background for the covenant purification imagery of verses 10-11: the sprinkling of clean water from Yehezkel 36:25-27 — I will sprinkle clean water on you — and the covenant new spirit and new heart. The Community Rule's covenant eschatological covenant purification by a holy spirit is the covenant community's covenant appropriation of the Yehezkelian covenant new covenant promise as the covenant ultimate covenant resolution of the covenant Two Spirits covenant conflict within the covenant human covenant heart.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 13 — all the glory of Adam shall be theirs. There shall be no more deceit and all the works of falsehood shall be put to shame. The covenant Adamic covenant glory formula — which appears in both the Damascus Document and the Community Rule — is the covenant community's covenant most concentrated covenant eschatological covenant promise. The covenant restoration of Adam's covenant original covenant glory represents not merely the covenant forgiveness of covenant sin but the covenant full covenant recovery of the covenant original covenant human covenant dignity, knowledge, and covenant life that the covenant primordial covenant transgression forfeited.

THE COMMUNITY RULE — THE ORDINANCES — COLUMNS 5 THROUGH 9

COMMUNITY RULE — COLUMN 5 The Rule for the Men of the Community — They Shall Separate From the Congregation of the Men of Falsehood — Anyone Who Refuses to Enter the Covenant Shall Not Enter the Water — He Shall Not Touch the Pure Meal — The Sons of Tzadok Are the Priests Who Keep the Covenant

1 And this is the rule for the men of the community who freely volunteer to turn from all evil and hold fast to all that he has commanded according to his will.

2 They shall separate from the congregation of the men of falsehood. They shall become a community in Torah and in property. They shall answer under the authority of the sons of Tzadok, the priests who keep the covenant.

3 And under the authority of the multitude of the men of the community who hold fast to the covenant: on their authority the decision shall be taken in every matter of Torah and property and justice.

4 To practice truth together and humility and righteousness and justice and loving-kindness. And to walk humbly in all their ways. No man shall walk in the stubbornness of his heart to go astray after his heart and his eyes and the inclination of his impulse.

5 Rather he shall circumcise in the community the foreskin of his impulse and his stiff neck to lay a foundation of truth for Yisra'EL, for the community of the everlasting covenant.

6 To atone for all those who freely volunteer for holiness in Aharon and for the house of truth in Yisra'EL and for those who join with them for community. And for controversy and judgment to condemn all those who transgress the statute.

7 These are the men of the foundation of the community: ten men, masters of wisdom, intelligence, and knowledge. One priest shall be at their head.

8 According to his authority shall they all speak. And no man of the congregation shall speak what is not pleasing to the many. And no man of the community shall speak before his turn.

9 And anyone who volunteers to join the council of the community shall be examined concerning his understanding and his deeds by the Inspector of the many. And if he understands teaching and his deeds are blameless, let him enter the covenant to turn from all evil.

10 And he shall keep away from all the evil ways in accord with his free will. And the men of the community shall warn him in all his deeds. And he shall be examined for a full year before he enters the pure food of the many.

11 Let no man of the community go into the water to share in the purity of the holy men, for they are not cleansed unless they turn from their wickedness, for all those who transgress his word are unclean.

12 Let no man join himself with him in his work or in his property lest he lay on him guilty transgression. Rather, let him keep away from him in every matter.

13 For thus it is written: Keep away from every false thing. And any man of the men of the community who transgresses one word of the Torah of Mosheh, whether openly or through deceit, shall be expelled from the council of the community.

14 And shall never return. And no man of the holy men shall partake of his property or of his counsel in any matter whatsoever. But if he is a new member and he stumbles, the Inspector shall examine him.

15 And if he did wrong unintentionally, let him be separated from the pure meal and from the council for two years. If in his first year he walks blamelessly and he has repaired his deeds he may return to the pure food and the council.

16 But whoever sins intentionally shall not return and shall be expelled from the community forever. And all who enter the covenant of the community and transgress any word of the Torah shall be expelled from the community.

17 These are the rules by which the men of perfect holiness shall walk one with another. Every man who enters the council of holiness, who walks in the way of perfect holiness as commanded,

18 He shall separate himself from the men of deceit. They shall not mix with them in matters of Torah and property. And they shall not eat or drink anything of their property.

19 They shall not receive anything from their hands unless it is for a price. As it is written: Keep away from the man in whose nostrils is breath, for of what account is he?

20 For all those who are not reckoned in his covenant are to be separated together with all they have. And a holy man shall not lean upon any deeds of vanity.

21 For all those who do not know his covenant are vanity and all who despise his word he will destroy from the world. And all their works are defiled before him and all their property unclean.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The 1QS Cave 1 scroll confirms Column 5. The column opens the practical rule section of the Community Rule — transitioning from the theological foundation of Columns 1-4 to the concrete institutional regulations of the community's daily life. The covenant dual authority structure — sons of Tzadok the priests and the multitude of the men of the community — verse 2-3 — is the Community Rule's covenant foundational covenant governance structure. The covenant entry process described in verses 9-16 is the most detailed covenant initiation covenant procedure in the Qumran corpus.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez tradition does not preserve the Community Rule. The covenant separation from the congregation of the men of falsehood — verse 1-2 — reflects the covenant conviction that the covenant community's covenant covenant identity requires covenant visible covenant institutional covenant separation from those who do not share the covenant exact interpretation of the covenant Torah. This covenant separatist covenant ethos is grounded in the covenant prophetic covenant call to covenant holiness that both the Orit Ge'ez and the Qumran covenant tradition preserve.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic citation of verse 13 — keep away from every false thing — from Shemot 23:7 demonstrates the Community Rule's characteristic method of supporting its covenant institutional covenant regulations with covenant direct covenant Torah citation. The covenant passage is used to ground the covenant community's covenant policy of separation from those outside the covenant in the covenant authority of the covenant Mosaic covenant Torah itself.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 11 — let no man of the community go into the water to share in the purity of the holy men, for they are not cleansed unless they turn from their wickedness, for all those who transgress his word are unclean. The covenant theology of covenant purification in the Community Rule is fundamentally covenant moral rather than merely covenant ritual: the covenant water does not cleanse the covenant man who has not first turned from his covenant wickedness. Covenant ritual covenant purity without covenant moral covenant repentance is covenant invalid. This covenant principle distinguishes the Qumran covenant community from covenant purely ritualistic covenant approaches to covenant purification.

COMMUNITY RULE — COLUMN 6 The Rule for the Session of the Many — None Shall Speak Before His Turn — The Novice Shall Not Touch the Pure Meal — In His Second Year He May Touch the Pure Meal — In His Third Year He May Touch the Drink of the Many — The Property of the Many

1 And this is the rule for the session of the many. Each man shall sit in his place. The priests shall sit first and the elders shall sit second and all the rest of the people shall each sit in his place.

2 And thus shall they be consulted in every matter. And the priest who is appointed over the many shall speak first, whatever he has to say. And then the elders shall speak. And then all the rest of the people.

3 Each man shall speak in his turn. And no man shall speak in the midst of the words of his neighbor before his brother has finished speaking. And no man shall speak before his appointed turn.

4 No boy shall enter among the assembly of the many. No man who is smitten with any impurity of men shall enter the assembly of the many. And the man who is smitten with any of these shall be separated from the congregation.

5 And this is the rule when the many assemble together: each man shall come as he is called. And the priests shall sit first, and the elders second, and all the rest of the people shall sit each man in his place.

6 And if there is a word of instruction from anyone, let him stand up and say: I have something to say to the many. And if they permit him he shall speak.

7 And the Inspector over the many shall be between thirty and fifty years of age and shall be master of every secret of men and every language of all their different nations.

8 According to his word shall the members of the community come in and go out, each according to his rank. And no one shall be promoted or demoted according to his own judgment.

9 There shall not be in the community any man who causes dissension. And no man shall speak a word against the Inspector of the many or the priest appointed over the many in a tone of contention.

10 Anyone who speaks or acts against any of the priests enrolled in the book shall be penalized for one year and be separated from the pure food of the many.

11 A man shall rebuke his neighbor in truth and humility and brotherly love. And no man shall speak to his brother in anger or in a complaining tone.

12 No man shall be enrolled in the council of the community if he has not been examined concerning his spirit, his deeds, and his understanding, whether he has walked blamelessly, for a full year. And if it seems good to enter the council of the community, the Inspector shall examine him.

13 And he shall be enrolled in the order of his rank among his brothers. And thereafter he shall not be counted among the many concerning Torah and property. And he may not partake of the pure meal of the many until he has been examined for a full year.

14 During his second year he shall be examined by the many. And if the many decide to admit him to the community, his property and earnings shall be handed over to the Inspector of the earnings of the many.

15 And they shall be entered to his credit but shall not be used for the benefit of the many. The man shall not touch the drink of the many until he has completed a second year among the men of the community.

16 When he has completed his second year, the many shall examine him. If the lot falls to him to enter the community, he shall be enrolled in the order of the community among his brothers.

17 And his property and earnings shall be merged with those of the many. And from that time on he shall be eligible to give testimony for the many, to adjudicate the community regulations, and to attend the pure meal with them.

18 These are the rules by which they shall act concerning the many in all matters related to money. The Inspector shall receive the wages of the many and judge all their cases and determine for them each the measure of his work.

19 And they shall give from their earnings two days' wages each month into the hand of the Inspector and the judges. And from it they shall give for the orphan and for the poor man and for the old man who is dying and for the wanderer.

20 And any man from the community who is deceived in money or property and takes it in full knowledge shall be separated from the pure food of the many for a year and shall be penalized for a quarter of his food ration.

21 And whoever answers his neighbor with a stiff neck or speaks to him with impatience, putting aside the foundation of the community to walk in the stubbornness of his heart, shall be penalized for one year.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The 1QS Cave 1 scroll confirms Column 6. The covenant two-year novitiate described in verses 12-17 is the most detailed covenant initiation process in the Qumran literature: during the first year the novice is examined but does not touch the pure meal; during the second year he is examined again and if accepted hands over his property but does not touch the drink; only after completing the second year and passing a third examination is he fully enrolled with access to both the pure meal and the community's drink. The covenant process reflects the covenant community's covenant profound covenant seriousness about the covenant integrity of its covenant membership.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez tradition does not preserve the Community Rule. The covenant communal property arrangement of verses 14-17 — in which the novice's property is eventually merged with the community's common fund — reflects a covenant economic covenant structure of covenant mutual covenant support that has parallels in the covenant Mosaic covenant legislation on the covenant Jubilee year and the covenant care for the covenant poor that the Orit Ge'ez preserves.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic tradition provides the covenant foundational covenant social covenant obligations — care for orphans, the poor, the elderly, and the wanderer — that the Community Rule institutionalizes in verses 19. The covenant two-days-wages monthly contribution — the same amount specified in the Damascus Document — reflects the covenant community's covenant systematic covenant approach to the covenant Mosaic covenant social covenant obligations.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 7-8 — the Inspector shall be between thirty and fifty years of age and shall be master of every secret of men and every language. According to his word shall the members come in and go out. The covenant age range for the Inspector in the Community Rule — thirty to fifty — matches the covenant Levitical covenant service age range from Bamidbar 4:3. The covenant community understands the Inspector's covenant role as functionally equivalent to the covenant Levitical covenant service: both are covenant appointed roles with covenant specific covenant age requirements and covenant specific covenant responsibilities before the covenant covenant community.

COMMUNITY RULE — COLUMN 7 The Penal Code — Whoever Lies Regarding His Property — Whoever Bears a Grudge — Whoever Speaks Foolishness — Whoever Falls Asleep During the Session — Whoever Puts Out His Hand to Strike His Fellow — Whoever Abandons the Community Shall Not Return

1 And whoever lies about his property knowingly shall be penalized for six months. And anyone who deliberately insults his neighbor shall be penalized for one year and be separated from the pure food of the many.

2 And whoever speaks about his neighbor something contrary to the truth with deceit shall be penalized for six months. And whoever bears a grudge against his neighbor without cause shall be penalized for six months to one year.

3 And whoever speaks a word of foolishness with his mouth, three months. And whoever speaks during the session of the many, ten days.

4 And whoever goes out during the session of the many without permission of the many or without urgent cause, ten days. And whoever falls asleep three times during the session of the many, he shall be penalized for ten days.

5 And whoever falls asleep during the session of the many, twenty days. And whoever goes away from the session and leaves without permission and leaves thirty times, he shall be penalized for one year.

6 And whoever walks before his neighbor completely naked when not necessary shall be penalized for six months. And whoever pulls out his hand from under his garment and exposes his nakedness shall be penalized for thirty days.

7 And whoever laughs foolishly and loudly shall be penalized for thirty days. And whoever draws out his left hand to gesticulate shall be penalized for ten days.

8 And whoever goes to slander his neighbor shall be separated from the pure food of the many for one year and penalized. And whoever slanders the many shall be expelled from them and shall not return.

9 And whoever murmurs against the institution of the community shall be expelled and not return. And whoever murmurs against the mouth of his neighbor unjustly shall be penalized for six months.

10 And if a man's spirit wavers from the foundation of the community to the point of treachery against truth and walks in the stubbornness of his heart, if he repents he shall be penalized for two years.

11 In the first year he shall not touch the pure food of the many. And in the second year he shall not touch the drink of the many. And he shall sit after all the men of the community in his rank.

12 When his two years are complete, the many shall examine him. If the lot falls to him, let him be enrolled in his place. But he shall not be trusted with any matter of Torah or property.

13 And whoever has been in the council of the community for ten years and whose spirit turns back to walk in the treachery of the wicked and transgresses the covenant shall be expelled and shall not return.

14 And no man of the holy men shall partake in his property or his counsel in any matter. But if he has been simple-minded and acted in deceit let him return to the examination of the many.

15 And whoever puts out his hand to strike his neighbor in anger shall be penalized for six months. And whoever strikes his neighbor by deceit shall be penalized for one year.

16 And whoever speaks the name of the Honored One — which is feared and terrible — whether in cursing or as a shock while reading the book or while blessing, shall be expelled and not return to the council of the community.

17 And whoever speaks in anger against one of the priests inscribed in the book shall be penalized for one year and separated from the pure food of the many.

18 And anyone who deceives the many in any matter, speaking without knowledge — if it comes to light — he shall be penalized for six months.

19 And whoever holds a grudge against his neighbor without justification — which is the spirit of Belial — shall be penalized for one year.

20 And whoever speaks a vain word into the mouth of any man shall be penalized for three months. And whoever puts a false claim before his neighbor shall be penalized for six months.

21 These are the regulations which the men of the community shall observe, each man with his neighbor, in the council of the community. They shall be judged by these regulations in every session of the many until ten men complete the community council in truth.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The 1QS Cave 1 scroll confirms Column 7, the Penal Code of the Community Rule. This is the most detailed covenant disciplinary code in the Qumran corpus — a systematic covenant catalog of covenant offences and their covenant corresponding covenant penalties that reveals the covenant community's covenant meticulous covenant attention to covenant behavioral covenant standards in the covenant common life. The covenant penalties range from ten days to permanent expulsion, with most offences receiving penalties measured in months or years of separation from the covenant pure meal and the covenant community's council.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez tradition does not preserve the Community Rule. The covenant prohibition against speaking the Honored Name — verse 16 — reflects the covenant community's covenant intense covenant reverence for the covenant divine covenant name that parallels the Orit Ge'ez tradition's own covenant careful covenant treatment of the divine covenant name in liturgical and covenant legal contexts. The covenant expulsion for speaking the covenant name inappropriately is the most severe covenant penalty in the Penal Code for a non-structural covenant offence.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic Vayikra 24:16 provides the covenant foundational covenant legal covenant basis for the covenant prohibition in verse 16. The Damascus Document and the Community Rule both apply the covenant Torah's covenant prohibition against blaspheming the name to the covenant specific covenant communal context of covenant public reading, blessing, and cursing — extending the covenant general covenant prohibition to the covenant specific covenant liturgical covenant situations where the covenant divine covenant name might be uttered.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 13 — whoever has been in the council of the community for ten years and whose spirit turns back to walk in the treachery of the wicked — shall be expelled and shall not return. The covenant ten-year threshold is the most striking element of the Community Rule's Penal Code: a man who has been fully enrolled in the community for ten years but then apostatizes receives a more severe judgment than a novice who transgresses — permanent expulsion without the possibility of return. The covenant length of covenant membership increases rather than decreases the covenant gravity of covenant apostasy.

COMMUNITY RULE — COLUMN 8 In the Council of the Community There Shall Be Twelve Men — They Shall Preserve Faith in the Land — The Community Shall Separate Into the Wilderness — They Shall Study the Torah Which He Commanded Through Mosheh — This Is the Study of the Torah — The Maskil Shall Not Rebuke the Men of the Pit

1 In the council of the community there shall be twelve men and three priests, blameless in all that is revealed of the whole Torah, to practice truth and righteousness and justice and loving-kindness and walking humbly, each with his neighbor.

2 To preserve faithfulness in the land with firm purpose and a broken spirit and to atone for iniquity by practicing justice and to suffer the distress of affliction.

3 To walk with all men according to the standard of truth and the rule of the age. When these exist in Yisra'EL, the council of the community shall be established in truth as an eternal planting, a holy house for Yisra'EL.

4 A most holy council for Aharon. True witnesses for the judgment and the chosen ones of his will to atone for the land and to render to the wicked their recompense.

5 This shall be the tested wall, the precious cornerstone. Its foundations shall not be shaken or moved from their place. A most holy dwelling for Aharon with everlasting knowledge for the covenant of justice.

6 And it shall offer to Creator a pleasing aroma and it shall be a house of perfection and truth in Yisra'EL to establish the covenant according to everlasting ordinances.

7 And they shall be an acceptable offering atoning for the land and deciding the judgment of wickedness and there shall be no more perversity.

8 When these have been established in the foundation of the community for two complete years in perfect conduct, they shall be set apart as holy within the council of the men of the community.

9 And any matter which was hidden from Yisra'EL but which has been found by the Seeker, he shall not keep it from them for fear of an apostate spirit.

10 And when these exist in Yisra'EL according to all these rules, they shall separate from the dwelling of the men of deceit and shall go to the wilderness to prepare there the way of the Creator.

11 As it is written: In the wilderness prepare the way of YHWH. Make straight in the desert a highway for our Creator.

12 This is the study of the Torah which he commanded through Mosheh to do according to all that has been revealed from time to time and according to what the prophets have revealed through his holy spirit.

13 And no man of the men of the community, the covenant of the community, who turns aside from a single commandment of the Torah of Mosheh, whether openly or deceitfully, shall touch the pure food of the holy men.

14 And he shall not know anything of their counsel until his works are purified from all deceit and he walks in perfection of conduct. And then he shall be admitted to the council according to the authority of the many and afterward he shall be enrolled in his rank.

15 According to this rule shall it be for everyone who joins the community. And these are the rules for the Maskil to walk in with every living man according to the measure for each time and the weight of each man.

16 He shall do the will of Creator according to all that has been revealed from time to time. And he shall learn all the wisdom that has been found according to the times.

17 And he shall set apart and weigh the sons of Tzadok according to their spirits. And he shall strengthen the chosen ones of the time according to his will, as he has commanded.

18 According to each man's spirit shall he do to him. He shall admit him according to the cleanness of his hands and advance him according to his understanding and thus shall his love be with his hate.

19 There shall be no rebuke or quarreling with the men of the Pit. And he shall conceal the counsel of the Torah in the midst of the men of deceit.

20 He shall rebuke in truth and righteous judgment those who choose the way. And he shall strengthen their steps in the holy way and in the truth of the everlasting precepts.

21 He shall guide them in the knowledge of the Most High and in the wisdom of the sons of heaven. For the one who walks blamelessly in all the ways of Creator they shall be as the testimony of truth as the truth of the everlasting precepts.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The 1QS Cave 1 scroll confirms Column 8. The covenant council of twelve men and three priests — verse 1 — is the covenant governing body of the covenant community: twelve representing the twelve tribes of Yisra'EL and three representing the three priestly divisions. The covenant citation of Yesha'yahu 40:3 in verse 11 — in the wilderness prepare the way of YHWH — is the Qumran community's covenant self-understanding in its most concentrated covenant form: the covenant community's covenant separation to the wilderness is the covenant literal covenant fulfillment of the covenant prophetic covenant call to covenant prepare the covenant way.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Yesha'yahu in full — including the covenant passage cited in verse 11. The Orit Ge'ez Ethiopian canonical tradition's preservation of the covenant prophetic covenant texts gives the covenant citation of Yesha'yahu 40:3 in the Community Rule an immediate covenant resonance with the covenant broader Afro-Asiatic covenant canonical covenant tradition that the entire project draws upon.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic Yesha'yahu 40:3 provides the covenant foundational covenant proof-text for the covenant community's covenant wilderness separation — verse 11. The covenant citation is the most theologically significant covenant scriptural covenant citation in the Community Rule: it grounds the covenant community's covenant institutional covenant choice — to separate to the wilderness — in the covenant authority of the covenant greatest covenant prophet of the covenant exile. The covenant wilderness is not merely a covenant geographical covenant choice but a covenant fulfillment of covenant prophecy.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 3-6 — the council of the community shall be established in truth as an eternal planting, a holy house for Yisra'EL, a most holy council for Aharon, true witnesses for the judgment, a tested wall, the precious cornerstone — when these exist in Yisra'EL it shall offer to Creator a pleasing aroma. The covenant community's covenant self-understanding as the covenant substitute for the covenant corrupt covenant temple and its covenant sacrificial covenant system is the covenant most theologically significant covenant institutional covenant claim in the Community Rule: the covenant council's covenant blameless covenant conduct replaces the covenant temple's covenant burnt offerings as the covenant acceptable covenant aroma before Creator.

COMMUNITY RULE — COLUMN 9 When These Exist the Holy Spirit Shall Be Atoned — The Men of Perfect Holiness — These Are the Rules for the Maskil — He Shall Conceal His Knowledge — The Appointed Times of Creator — The Maskil Shall Walk in Wisdom — He Shall Bless His Maker

1 When these exist in Yisra'EL according to all these rules for a foundation of the holy spirit in everlasting truth, to atone for the guilt of transgression and the faithlessness of sin and for acceptance of the land without the flesh of burnt offerings and without the fat of sacrifice.

2 The offering of the lips in compliance with the decree shall be as a righteous fragrance, and the perfection of the way shall be as the voluntary offering of a pleasing oblation.

3 At that time the men of the community shall separate themselves as a holy house for Aharon to be united as a most holy and as a house of the community for Yisra'EL who walk in perfection.

4 Only the sons of Aharon shall administer judgment and property, and according to their mouth shall the lot fall for every rule of the men of the community.

5 And the property of the holy men who walk in perfection shall not be mixed with the property of the men of deceit who have not purified their way by separating themselves from iniquity and by walking in perfect conduct.

6 They shall not depart from any counsel of the Torah to walk in all the stubbornness of their heart, but shall be ruled by the primitive rules in which the men of the community were instructed at the beginning.

7 Until there shall come the prophet and the Messiahs of Aharon and Yisra'EL. These are the statutes for the Maskil in which he shall walk with every living being according to the rule of each time and the weight of each man.

8 He shall do the will of Creator as he has commanded through Mosheh and through all his servants the prophets. And he shall love all that he has chosen and hate all that he has rejected.

9 He shall keep himself far from all evil and he shall cleave to all good deeds. And he shall practice truth and righteousness and justice in the land and shall no longer walk in the stubbornness of a guilty heart.

10 And all who walk in these ways shall be acceptable as covenant members of the community forever. He shall search and probe the ordinances according to the spirits, distinguishing according to the measure of each time.

11 He shall love all the sons of light, each according to his lot in Creator's design, and shall hate all the sons of darkness, each according to his guilt in the vengeance of Creator.

12 He shall not turn aside from any command of Creator regarding their appointed times. He shall not advance their appointed times nor fall behind them. He shall not deviate to the right or to the left from any of his true words.

13 He shall walk prudently in all his ways with the deceit of the wicked world, concealing the knowledge of the truth and the counsel of the Torah among the men of deceit.

14 He shall rebuke with truthful knowledge and righteous judgment all those who choose the way, each according to his spirit, according to the rule of the time.

15 He shall guide them in knowledge and instruct them in the mysteries of wonder and truth among the members of the community that they may walk blamelessly each with his neighbor in all that has been revealed to them.

16 This is the time for making way in the wilderness and he shall instruct them in all that has been found necessary to do in this time to separate from every man who has not turned his way from all wickedness.

17 These are the rules of conduct for the Maskil in these times concerning his love and his hatred: everlasting hatred in a spirit of secrecy for the men of the Pit.

18 He shall leave to them wealth and profit and labor of his hands as a slave does to his master and as the humble toward his ruler, and he shall be a man zealous for the statute and its appointed time, for the day of vengeance.

19 He shall do the will of Creator in all the works of his hands and in all his dominion as he commanded. And in everything that befalls him he shall delight and nothing that befalls him shall displease him apart from the will of Creator.

20 In the mouth of all the sons of truth he shall take delight. And the words of his mouth shall be truth, and with all the living he shall walk in the spirit of the knowledge of the covenant.

21 All his love shall be for all the sons of truth and all the sons of light. And his hatred shall be an everlasting hatred for all the men of the Pit.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The 1QS Cave 1 scroll confirms Column 9. The covenant substitute-sacrifice theology of verses 1-2 — the offering of the lips as a righteous fragrance and the perfection of the way as a pleasing oblation — is the most theologically developed covenant statement of the Community Rule's covenant substitution of covenant communal covenant worship for the covenant temple's covenant sacrificial covenant system. The covenant community's covenant prayer and covenant blameless covenant conduct are not merely equivalent to covenant sacrifice — they are the covenant authentic covenant sacrifice that the covenant corrupt covenant temple can no longer perform.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez tradition preserves the covenant prophetic covenant critique of covenant hollow covenant sacrifice — notably in the Yesha'yahu and Amos material — that forms the covenant theological covenant background for the Community Rule's covenant substitute-sacrifice theology. The covenant prophetic covenant insistence that Creator desires covenant obedience rather than covenant sacrifice — Shemu'el Aleph 15:22 — grounds the covenant community's covenant conviction that covenant blameless covenant living is more acceptable than covenant corrupted covenant temple covenant worship.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic Yesha'yahu 1:11-17, Amos 5:21-24, and Mika 6:6-8 provide the covenant prophetic covenant foundation for the covenant substitute-sacrifice theology of Column 9. The covenant community's covenant conviction that Creator prefers covenant right conduct to covenant formal covenant sacrifice is not a covenant novel covenant theological covenant position but the covenant application of the covenant established covenant prophetic covenant tradition to the covenant specific covenant historical covenant situation of a covenant community separated from the covenant temple.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 7 — until there shall come the prophet and the Messiahs of Aharon and Yisra'EL. The covenant triple eschatological covenant expectation — a prophet and two Messiahs — is the Community Rule's covenant most explicit covenant messianic covenant statement. The covenant prophet who comes before the covenant Messiahs echoes Devarim 18:15-18 and Malakhi 4:5. The covenant dual Messiahs — of Aharon and of Yisra'EL — corresponds to the Damascus Document's covenant dual-Messiah expectation. Together the three covenant eschatological covenant figures represent the covenant prophet, priest, and king of the covenant community's covenant eschatological covenant hope.

THE COMMUNITY RULE — THE HYMNS OF THE MASKIL — COLUMNS 10 THROUGH 11

COMMUNITY RULE — COLUMN 10 I Will Bless His Name — At All the Appointed Times Which He Ordained — I Will Sing With the Knowledge of All My Lips — I Will Not Repay Evil for Evil — I Will Not Envy the Spirit of Violence — I Will Open My Mouth in a Hymn of Praise — Bless the Creator of All

1 I will bless his name. And I will recount his glory with the tongue of flesh. With the tongue of knowledge I will bless him. I will praise him at the beginning of the dominion of light and at its turning when it withdraws to its appointed dwelling.

2 At the beginning of the watches of darkness when he opens their storehouse and spreads them out over the height, and at their turning when they withdraw before the light.

3 When luminaries appear from the dwelling of holiness and when they withdraw to the place of glory. At the entry of the appointed times in the days of the new month together with their periods and when they change, each for the other.

4 The renewal of the great light when he turns to the holy of holies, the season of the year and its periods, one for another. And at the junction of the Jubilees, the statute of the appointed years of the Sabbath of the Shabbatot.

5 I will bless him with the offering of what comes from my lips, as a prescribed statute engraved forever: in the beginning of the year and at the turn of its seasons and at the fulfillment of their decrees in their appointed order.

6 One for the other: the period of harvest to summer and the period of sowing to the period of grass, the periods of years to their weeks and at the beginning of their weeks for the season of release.

7 All my life the engraved statute shall be on my tongue as a fruit of praise and as an offering of my lips. I will sing with knowledge and all my music shall be for the glory of Creator.

8 I will play my harp according to his holy order and the pipe of my lips I will tune to his righteous rule. With the coming of day and night I will enter the covenant of Creator. And with the going out of evening and morning I will speak his laws.

9 And where he has set their limit I shall not turn back. His just judgment I will pronounce concerning my perversity and my transgressions shall be before my eyes as an engraved statute.

10 To Creator I will say: My righteousness and to the Most High: Foundation of my goodness. Source of knowledge, Spring of holiness, Height of glory, Almighty of eternal majesty.

11 I will choose that which he teaches me and will delight in his judgment of me. Whenever I begin to stretch out my hands and my feet, I will bless his name. When I go out and when I come in and sit and rise and lie on my bed I will exult in him.

12 I will bless him with the offering of what comes from my lips in the ranks of men and before I stretch out my hands to eat the fat of the fruits of the earth.

13 In the beginning of fear and dread and in the place of distress and desolation I will bless him. I will give him thanks for all his wonderful deeds and meditate on his power and lean upon his compassion all day long.

14 For I know that in his hand is the justice for every living man and all his deeds are truth. Whenever distress is loosed I will praise him and at his salvation I will also exult.

15 I will not repay any man with evil's recompense. I will pursue every man with good, for with Creator is the judgment of every living man and he it is who will repay to every man his recompense.

16 I will not envy in a wicked spirit and my soul shall not covet the wealth of violence. I will not grapple with the men of the Pit until the day of vengeance.

17 But my anger I will not turn back from the men of deceit and I will not be content until he establishes judgment. I will bear no grudge against those who repent of transgression but have no compassion on all who turn aside from the way.

18 I will not comfort those smitten of spirit but will not support the strength of evil. Righteousness shall be for me a girdle of the reins and faithfulness a robe of justice.

19 Blessed is Creator who does great wonders and keeps his covenant with those who keep his commands. In the mouth of all the living I will bless him for he it is who has taught me his wonders and his glory and in the mouth of his servants I will proclaim them.

20 And his ways are paths of justice and of truth. His works are perfect and founded on truth. And his judgments are truth and righteousness and his mercy is steadfast love with all who do his will.

21 I will open my mouth as a spring of joy and my tongue as a fruit of righteousness. And I will make my lips a hymn of praise. I will sing his knowledge and all my music shall be for the glory of Creator.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The 1QS Cave 1 scroll confirms Column 10, the great Hymn of the Maskil. The covenant transition from covenant institutional covenant legislation to covenant covenant poetry is the most striking structural movement in the Community Rule: after nine columns of covenant community regulations, the covenant text breaks into covenant magnificent covenant liturgical covenant poetry. The covenant Maskil's covenant hymn covers the covenant full span of the covenant calendar — the turning of light and darkness, the new moons, the Jubilees, the Shabbat of Shabbatot — framing covenant communal covenant worship within the covenant cosmic covenant order of the covenant created time.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez covenant tradition preserves the covenant calendar theology — Jubilees, weeks, appointed times — verse 4-6 — in the book of Yovelim which is part of the Orit Ge'ez canon. The Community Rule's covenant hymnic covenant engagement with the covenant calendar confirms the covenant deep covenant connection between the covenant Qumran covenant community's covenant liturgical covenant life and the covenant Jubilee covenant calendar tradition preserved in the Orit Ge'ez.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic Tehillim provide the covenant foundational covenant liturgical covenant framework for Column 10's covenant hymn. The covenant not-repaying-evil — verse 15 — echoes Tehillim 37 and its covenant counsel of covenant patient covenant waiting for Creator's covenant judgment. The covenant bless his name refrain that opens and closes the covenant hymn reflects the covenant characteristic Tehillim covenant doxological covenant structure.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 15 — I will not repay any man with evil's recompense. I will pursue every man with good, for with Creator is the judgment of every living man and he it is who will repay to every man his recompense. The covenant renunciation of personal covenant vengeance — embedded within a covenant community that practices covenant separation from the men of the Pit — is one of the most morally sophisticated covenant statements in the Qumran literature: the covenant community separates from covenant wicked men not out of covenant hatred that demands covenant personal covenant retaliation but out of covenant conviction that Creator's covenant judgment alone is the covenant appropriate covenant response to covenant evil.

COMMUNITY RULE — COLUMN 11 From the Source of His Righteousness Is My Justification — Creator Has Given to the Elect of Mankind an Everlasting Possession — He Has Joined Their Assembly to the Sons of Heaven — To the Ones He Loves He Has Given Everlasting Glory — As for Me I Belong to Wicked Mankind — My Justification Is With Creator

1 From the source of his righteousness is my justification and from his wondrous mysteries is the light in my heart. My eyes have gazed on that which is eternal, on wisdom concealed from men, on knowledge and wise design hidden from the sons of men.

2 A fountain of righteousness and a reservoir of strength and a spring of glory hidden from the assembly of flesh. To those whom Creator has chosen he has given them as an everlasting possession.

3 He has given them an inheritance in the lot of the holy ones and has joined their assembly with the sons of heaven to be a council of the community and a company of the holy building for an eternal planting throughout all ages.

4 As for me, I belong to wicked mankind, to the assembly of perverse flesh. My iniquities, my transgressions, my sins together with the wickedness of my heart belong to the assembly of worms and those who walk in darkness.

5 For the way of man is not established except by the spirit which Creator made for him to perfect a path for the sons of man that all his creatures might know the might of his power and the abundance of his compassion with all the sons of his will.

6 As for me, if I stumble, the mercies of Creator are my salvation always. And if I fall in the sin of the flesh, my justification shall be by the righteousness of Creator which endures forever.

7 If he brings my distress to light, from the pit he will draw my soul and direct my steps to the way. He will draw me near in his compassion and in his loving-kindness bring my justification.

8 He will judge me in the righteousness of his truth and in the greatness of his goodness always atone for all my sins. And in his righteousness he will purify me from the uncleanness of the human being and from the sin of the sons of man.

9 To the praise of Creator belongs his righteousness and to the Most High his majesty. Blessed are you, my Creator, who opens the heart of your servant to knowledge and directs all his works in righteousness.

10 And establishes for the son of your handmaid as you have willed with all those who love your name and keep your precepts — to stand before you forever.

11 No man is righteous before you when he enters into judgment, and no one is declared innocent in a court case before you. But as for man, one man is more righteous than another, a man is more knowledgeable than his fellow man.

12 But in all these things the spirit of flesh is not equal. As for me, my justification belongs to Creator and in his hand is the perfection of my way together with the uprightness of my heart.

13 In his righteousness my transgressions are blotted out. For from the fountain of his knowledge he has opened my light and my eyes have gazed on his wonders and the light of my heart on the mystery of that which shall be.

14 That which endures forever is the support of my right hand. On a rock of strength is the path of my steps. They shall not stagger before anything. For the truth of Creator is the rock I tread.

15 His might is the support of my right hand and from the spring of his righteousness is my justification. A light is in my heart from his marvelous mysteries and in the eternal being my eye has beheld that which is concealed from man.

16 Knowledge and wise design are hidden from the sons of man, a fountain of righteousness and a reservoir of might and a spring of glory concealed from the assembly of flesh.

17 To whom Creator has given them they possess forever and have inherited in the lot of the holy ones. He has joined their assembly to the sons of heaven in a council of the community.

18 And the company of the holy building for an everlasting planting throughout all ages to come. But I belong to wicked mankind to the assembly of perverse flesh.

19 My iniquities and my transgressions and my sins with the perversities of my heart belong to the assembly of worms and those who walk in darkness. For the way of man is not his own. A man cannot direct his steps.

20 For justification is with Creator and perfection of way is from his hand. By his knowledge all things exist and all that does exist he establishes by his purpose and without him nothing is done.

21 As for me, if I stumble, the mercies of Creator shall be my eternal salvation. If I stagger in fleshly iniquity, my justification shall be in the righteousness of Creator which stands forever. He has opened my distress with his light and given my eyes to see his ways. My feet stand on level ground in the assemblies I will bless his name.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] The 1QS Cave 1 scroll confirms Column 11, the concluding Hymn of Justification. This column is the theological summit of the Community Rule — a covenant personal covenant confession of covenant total covenant dependence on Creator's covenant righteousness for covenant justification, covenant purification, and covenant guidance. The covenant repeated refrain — my justification is with Creator — verse 2, 6, 12, 15, 20 — is the Community Rule's most concentrated covenant theological covenant statement: the covenant community member who has walked through nine columns of covenant community covenant regulations concludes with the covenant radical covenant acknowledgment that even the covenant blameless conduct the covenant regulations require is only possible through Creator's covenant righteousness.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez tradition does not preserve the Community Rule. However, the covenant theology of Column 11 — total covenant dependence on Creator's covenant righteousness for covenant justification, the covenant acknowledgment of covenant human covenant frailty — verse 4: as for me, I belong to wicked mankind — and the covenant conviction that no man is righteous before Creator when he enters into judgment — verse 11 — resonates with the covenant confessional covenant tradition preserved in the great prayers of Daniyel 9 and Nekhemyah 9 that the Orit Ge'ez canonical collection preserves.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic Tehillim — particularly Tehillim 143:2: do not enter into judgment with your servant, for no one living is righteous before you — provides the covenant direct covenant textual covenant background for verse 11. The Community Rule's covenant concluding covenant hymn draws on the covenant Tehillim covenant tradition of covenant total covenant dependence on Creator's covenant hesed — steadfast love — as the covenant ultimate covenant basis of covenant human covenant standing before the covenant divine covenant court.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 4 — as for me, I belong to wicked mankind, to the assembly of perverse flesh. My iniquities, my transgressions, my sins together with the wickedness of my heart belong to the assembly of worms and those who walk in darkness. The covenant paradox at the heart of the Community Rule: the covenant document that established the covenant strictest covenant community covenant standards in the covenant Qumran corpus concludes with the covenant Maskil himself — the covenant community's covenant highest covenant spiritual covenant authority — placing himself among wicked mankind and the assembly of worms. The covenant perfection the covenant regulations demand is grounded not in covenant human covenant achievement but in covenant total covenant dependence on Creator's covenant righteousness.

SEREKH HAYACHAD — COMPLETE

MY JUSTIFICATION IS WITH CREATOR

AND IN HIS HAND IS THE PERFECTION OF MY WAY
